label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to see if I follow these steps, do I end up back where I started? First, I take 7 steps forward. Let's say I'm facing north. So I'm at position 7 north of the starting point. Then I take 8 steps forward. Now I'm at 7 + 8 = 15 steps north of the start. Next, I take 10 steps forward. So I'm at 15 + 10 = 25 steps north. Then I turn around, which means I'm now facing south. Turn around again, so I'm back to facing north. Now I take 5 steps forward, so I'm at 25 + 5 = 30 steps north of the start. Then I turn around again, now facing south. So the final position is 30 steps north of the starting point. I'm facing south, but the question is whether I'm back at the starting point. Since I'm 30 steps north, I haven't returned to the starting point. Let me double-check the steps: 1. 7 steps forward: +7 2. 8 steps forward: +7 +8 = +15 3. 10 steps forward: +15 +10 = +25 4. Turn around (facing opposite direction) 5. Turn around again (facing original direction) 6. 5 steps forward: +25
